title: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms
display_title: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms
wikitext:
[]: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms             Quote Post by Ong Seng Keong » Sun Aug 13, 2023 2:31 am This will sound very out-of-nowhere but I’ve stated to notice that the school uniforms worn by Asuka, Rei, Hikari, and all the other girls in the Tokyo-3 First Municipal Middle School eerily resemble some of the uniforms worn by female members of the Communist Youth League of China. I’m curious if this was a deliberate design decision or just pure coincidence? Above: First girl on the left wealth the Communist Youth League of China uniform Above: Compare first image with what Asuka, Rei, and Hikari are wearing in the image just above this caption

[]: Re: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Sun Aug 13, 2023 3:24 am Interesting. It wouldn't be the first time communist aesthetic was used in a popular franchise; Akira Toriyama drew several of his Dragon Ball characters in uniforms reminiscent of both Chinese communist and Soviet military, and even drew himself in the same garb in the afterwords of the manga volumes.

[]: Re: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Sun Aug 13, 2023 2:13 pm Not that I know, though I do know some suits were modeled on Soviet cosmonauts. Sadamoto had an interview on it, and one on seifuku in general. I'll edit it in later. EDIT: Right, so a while ago I found that interview and put it on the wiki, gwern's only had incomplete snippets from one interview (there are several) since the page went down so I had to dug through archived versions at the time. https://wiki.evageeks.org/Statements_by ... .2F2010.29 Looking at it he doesn't seem to mention any specific influences, I'm afraid. In here he talks about the cosmonaut inspiration: https://gwern.net/doc/www/gainax.forumf ... 5124b.html For Mari's old plug suit, I combined a rich green that seemed to me very reminiscent of British clothing with the design aesthetic of the space suits the Soviets were using around the time of the Cold War. The Soviet space suits are the reason for the checkered pattern of the lower half of the suit

[]: Re: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun Aug 13, 2023 7:03 pm Huh, that's fascinating, I was not aware of that. I wouldn't think much of it, since it is still a pretty generic school uniform, but there is very likely a co-development in the real world uniforms that the character designs were based off

[]: Re: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms             Quote Post by Joshmilo » Wed Jun 26, 2024 12:28 pm Interesting observation about the school uniforms in "Neon Genesis Evangelion" resembling those of the Communist Youth League of China. It’s possible that the similarity is coincidental, as many school uniforms share common design elements globally. While we don’t have confirmation from the creators about the intention behind the uniform designs, it might be interesting to approach this from an analytical angle, similar to how we use a GWA calculator for academic performance. If we were to "grade" the effectiveness of uniform designs in conveying thematic elements of the series, such as authority and conformity, how would they score? This could offer a structured way to evaluate visual symbolism in "Evangelion."

[]: Re: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms             Quote Post by Asunji_Yuko » Thu Jun 27, 2024 7:51 pm Ong Seng Keong wrote: This will sound very out-of-nowhere but I’ve stated to notice that the school uniforms worn by Asuka, Rei, Hikari, and all the other girls in the Tokyo-3 First Municipal Middle School eerily resemble some of the uniforms worn by female members of the Communist Youth League of China. I’m curious if this was a deliberate design decision or just pure coincidence? Above: First girl on the left wealth the Communist Youth League of China uniform Above: Compare first image with what Asuka, Rei, and Hikari are wearing in the image just above this caption

[]: Re: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms             Quote Post by Javi2541997 » Wed Jul 24, 2024 12:35 pm No, I think not. They are just a big coincidence. School uniforms in Evangelion are a clear representation of real life Japanese students. It is called “gakuran” and it was implemented in the Meiji era with the basic purpose of instilling a sense of discipline and community. If I am not wrong, they are all the same in every high school in Japan and all students wear them, without taking into account their social status. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms             Quote Post by Gendos Glasses » Wed Jul 24, 2024 12:53 pm Javi2541997 wrote: No, I think not. They are just a big coincidence. School uniforms in Evangelion are a clear representation of real life Japanese students. It is called “gakuran” and it was implemented in the Meiji era with the basic purpose of instilling a sense of discipline and community. If I am not wrong, they are all the same in every high school in Japan and all students wear them, without taking into account their social status. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms             Quote Post by Javi2541997 » Thu Jul 25, 2024 7:57 am Gendos Glasses wrote: The gakuran is the Japanese school uniform for the male students, but this topic is about the female uniforms.

[]: Re: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms             Quote Post by dzzthink » Thu Jul 25, 2024 12:10 pm If you watch Suzume and Your Name you see the same red tie ribbon, so I'm guessing the red ribbon is actually a common type of Japanese school uniform. Regarding the rest of the school uniform (excluding the red ribbon), I am not too sure but you could certainly make the case that they are more European-influenced ( https://histclo.com/schun/country/eng/gen/eg-chron.html ) or Euro-Japanese style that is common in Japan (Wikipedia has an entry on how it is modelled after British naval uniforms https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/School_uniforms_in_Japan ). Chinese school children (male and female) wear red ribbons because they represent the Communist Youth League, so it's a political symbol. However, I believe they are much thicker and shorter than what we see in Evangelion, and they are more like pieces of cloth that you wrap around your neck with a simple knot (and not the butterfly knot that we see in Evangelion).

Article: Are the female school uniforms in the Evangelion franchise based on Chinese communist uniforms
 Your Name you see the same red tie ribbon, so I'm guessing the red ribbon is actually a common type of Japanese school uniform. Regarding the rest of the school uniform (excluding the red ribbon), I am not too sure but you could certainly make the case that they are more European-influenced ( https://histclo.com/schun/country/eng/gen/eg-chron.html ) or Euro-Japanese style that is common in Japan (Wikipedia has an entry on how it is modelled after British naval uniforms https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/School_uniforms_in_Japan ). Chinese school children (male and female) wear red ribbons because they represent the Communist Youth League, so it's a political symbol. However, I believe they are much thicker and shorter than what we see in Evangelion, and they are more like pieces of cloth that you wrap around your neck with a simple knot (and not the butterfly knot that we see in Evangelion).
